div1.entryFree:
Blister, vb., 1) trans. to cover with blisters: a southwest blow on ye and b. you all o'er, Tp. I, 2, 324. a gentlewoman who, falling in the flaws of her own youth, hath --ed her report, Meas. II, 3, 12. --ed be thy tongue for such a wish, Rom. III, 2, 90. this tyrant whose sole name --s our tongues, Mcb. IV, 3, 12. In Lr. II, 4, 170 Ff to fall and b., Qq to fall and blast her pride.
2) intr. to rise in blisters: if I prove honeymouthed, let my tongue b. Wint. II, 2, 33.
